title: lupinus arbustus subsp. neolaxiflorus
intro: Lupinus arbustus subsp. neolaxiflorus, also known as the shrubby lupine, is a subspecies of the lupine plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This perennial legume has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a useful remedy for conditions such as diabetes, arthritis, and kidney stones. Lupinus arbustus subsp. neolaxiflorus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
